title: Apologia pro Vita Sua
content:
The poet in his lone yet genial hour
    Gives to his eyes a magnifying power:
    Or rather he emancipates his eyes
    From the black shapeless accidents of size -
    In unctuous cones of kindling coal,
    Or smoke upwreathing from the pipe's trim bole,
            His gifted ken can see
            Phantoms of sublimity.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Samuel Taylor Coleridge, titled "Apologia pro Vita Sua", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
